subject: Urology
title: Varicocele: Why It Is Always Left
hook: A bag of worms that empties when he lies down, and almost always on the left. Here is why.

points.heading: The classic vignette
points.body: A varicocele is dilated pampiniform plexus veins felt as a bag of worms. It empties on lying down and on elevating the scrotum, and reappears on standing.

points.heading: Why the left side dominates
points.body: The left testicular vein is longer and joins the left renal vein at a right angle, raising pressure. The left renal vein can also be compressed between the aorta and SMA, the nutcracker effect.

points.heading: The number that defines it
points.body: Normal pampiniform veins measure about 0.5 to 1.5 mm. A varicocele is diagnosed when the diameter exceeds 2 mm. Confirm with colour Doppler while standing or on Valsalva.

points.heading: Fertility and management
points.body: Raised scrotal temperature impairs spermatogenesis and can cause oligospermia. Varicocele is found in 15 to 20% of men but around 40% of infertile men. Asymptomatic cases need only reassurance and support.

caption: Varicocele is a guaranteed mark if you can explain why it is almost always left: a longer vein, a right-angle renal entry, and the nutcracker compression between aorta and SMA. Add the bag of worms that empties on lying down and the 2 mm cutoff, and the vignette writes itself.
